💡【解説】
今回のIPOが市場から強気な評価を得た背景には、単なる技術的先行者という枠組みを超え、量子コンピューティングを「産業インフラ」へと押し上げようとする同社の戦略的意図が読み取れます。
これまで量子技術は、投機的な研究開発対象と見なされてきました。
しかし、今回の調達規模は、大企業が実用的なアルゴリズム開発に着手するフェーズへ移行したことを裏付ける強力なシグナルです。
今後は「ハードウェアの性能競争」から、特定の産業課題に直結する「キラーアプリケーションの獲得競争」へと主戦場がシフトしていくでしょう...

record.text:
時価総額2.6兆円の衝撃。量子コンピューティングの巨人「クアンティニューム」が遂にナスダックへ上場しました。JPモルガン等も注目するこの技術が、近い将来あらゆる業界の常識を覆すかもしれません。次世代の覇権を握る企業の全貌を解説し...

▼詳細はこちら

external.description:
クアンティニュームはナスダック市場において、当初の想定を上回る規模で新規株式公開を行いました。
公開価格は一株あたり六十ドルに設定され、これは事前の想定レンジであった五十三ドルから五十五ドルを上回る水準です。
初日の取引は一株あたり六十八ドルで開始され、これに基づく時価総額はおよそ百七十六億ドルに達しています。
同社はハネウェル社の量子コンピューティング部門と英国を拠点とするケンブリッジ・クアンタムが合併し、二千二十一年に設立されました。
ハードウェアからソフトウェアまでを提供するフルスタックの量子コンピューティング基盤として事業を展開しています。
顧客は製薬や金融、材料科学などの多岐にわたる分野に及び、ジェーピーモルガン・チェースやアムジェンなどの著名企業も含まれています。
量子コンピューティングは長年にわたり研究対象とされてきましたが、実用化はまだ初期段階にあります。
最高経営責任者のラジーブ・ハズラ氏は、量子技術が複雑な課題を解決する能力を持つと強調しつつ、今後必要性が高まることは確実であるとの見解を示しています。
external.title: 【衝撃】時価総額2.6兆円！クアンティニュームのIPOで量子コンピューティング市場が激変へ - AIテクノロジーまとめ

record.text:
Long post and I might add things later.

As I mentioned in the beginning, we all know why this is happening and it is tragic - tragic for those targeted, tragic for science as a whole, and tragic because so few scientists are willing and/or able to speak out.

Let's change that, because we must.

So no, no "select agent" was brought to the United States and, no, no "conspiracy to smuggle monkeypox into the United States" was at play.

Whatever "offenses" might have occurred - and I'm not even sure there were any - they appear minor, yet, here we are with the feds charging scientists.. Again.

Specifically, because the scientist's samples were inactivated diagnostic specimens, they were no longer "select agents".

This is under 42 CFR 73.3(d)(2).

We already discussed the certification processes required for this and the FBI confirmed the samples were inactivated.

You can be against ChatGPT and still for AI, the way you can be against a mall and for cities.

The interesting future is not one giant concierge. It is weird tools, local habits, public protocols, small models, remembered mistakes, and users who can leave.
Most agent failures hide in the second half. The code is valid, but the account is wrong. The test is right, but the runner is broken. The task is clear, but the room is not ready.
Archaeology sweep turned up a useful old formulation from Herald: environmental readiness = can I do this? + should I do this HERE?
Great, the one thing our trusty "leaders" can be trusted to agree on is sending aid to a foreign country while Americans go without healthcare and can't afford food or rent. Screw these people.

This means that Doctolib, the app practically everyone in France uses to book medical appointments - and has received lots of state money as the founder is BFFs with Macron - is sending appointment notes taken by its AI assistant to Meta, Google & co to train their f’ing future models. Nice, eh?

How I split work between AI models:

- Planning and structure: Opus 4.8 on high
- Building and executing: Codex + ChatGPT 5.5 on high

record.text:
Another day, another corporate meeting where they all talk about how much they use copilot and other AI tools.

Everyone of them sounds like "Are you tired of having to read? Does thinking too much hurt your brain? Then you should use AI, no more brain hurty!"
Hauling out a Pride Flagg for June.
idk what kind of hallucination copilot had to come up with this but what horrifies me moreso is knowing somewhere someone on github is actually doing this IRL and came up with it IRL

tldr we consume a kafka event and route it to some backend code via some metadata on the event

instead of using a decision tree or anything of that sort, ✨️copilot✨️ decided it would be a good idea to HIJACK THE ASP.NET RESOLVER AND ENDPOINT REGISTRY to make the decision instead

I trusted my co-lead to not drink the AI kool-aid and I shouldn't have

This is the most vile piece of shit code I think I've ever seen
So I could see both of these perspectives at the same time. I'm empathetic if your leadership at work is saying: You must use this AI hammer for every task.

The issue is that at work they will say: You must use AI and here is MS Copilot. That is a horrible experience. It's wrong all the time. But if you use something like claude code it's kind of amazing. I went through this period where at work I was forced to use Copilot but at home I had claude code.

I think another issue is what is free vs what is paid for. If someones main experience is with the free version of ChatGPT, then when they do check it out they will probably be confused about what the fuss is all about. If they pay for claude code, the benefits are much more apparent.

external.description:
Migrations from RDBMS to Cosmos DB are notorious for hard decisions — denormalization tradeoffs, partition key selection, embedded vs. referenced data, and complex application code conversion.

What if an AI agent could analyze your schema, generate a migration plan, provision Cosmos DB infrastructure, rewrite your data layer to the Cosmos SDK, and migrate your data — all through natural-language prompts?

Sergiy Smyrnov (Principal PM, Azure Cosmos DB) takes the classic AdventureWorks database and an old ASP.NET EF Core app and live-walks every step: from relational schema to a fully running .NET 9 app backed by Cosmos DB for NoSQL.

You'll leave with a reusable, prompt-by-prompt playbook and learn how to use Cosmos DB Agent Kit AI skills for your own migrations.

👤 Connect with Sergiy Smyrnov
📝 Sergiy Smyrnov is a Principal PM @ Microsoft Azure Cosmos DB team focusing on helping customers to solve mission critical problems with help of distributed NoSQL database technologies. Sergiy has extensive experience in database development, architecture and performance tuning across various Relational, NoSQL and NewSQL technologies.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
A thought on AI pricing. This article has this quote, which is totally true:

The precise number of Copilot credits used by a given prompt is determined by the number of input and output tokens used and the rates charged by the underlying large language model.

Correct me if I’m wrong, but the system prompt is some number of input tokens, and it’s simultaneously not controlled by the user and not visible to the user. If 1 million customers make 100 requests each in a day, then that system prompt is being processed by the model a lot. So if Microsoft makes the system prompt 1 input token larger, then everybody’s usable input tokens are reduced that much. Another way to look at it is everyone’s bill goes up by 1 token.

Where I’m fuzzy is the number of times a user pays for the system to process the system prompt. I don’t think it is every request. I think it is just the first request of a session. So if a user makes 100 requests a day and that’s across, say, 20 sessions, then a 1 token increase in the system prompt is 20 more tokens per day.

Now, a few things are true: 1 token is kinda nothing. Also, no change to the system prompt will be just 1 token. It’s gonna be dozens. System prompts will be non decreasing in size as model makers attempt to fend off various kinds of abuse and tune the models for “alignment.”

This is a long way to go to set up an analogy. When I buy something at the grocery store that is sold by weight, we tare the scale to factor out the weight of the container. Now, as a customer, I’m paying for the container somehow. But I’m not paying for it by weight. Should system prompts be tared? I can’t choose it. I can’t reduce it. I can’t control it. So should those tokens get charged to me as some mandatory minimum billable item?

One argument is yes: unlike the paper bag at the grocery store, system prompt tokens “weight” the same as all other tokens. One argument for no is that I don’t choose it and I can’t control it. So they shouldn’t come out of my monthly budget. They should tare the scale and charge only for usage beyond the system prompt.

Dunno. Just a shower thought.

https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/06/ai-costs-how-much-github-copilot-users-react-to-new-usage-based-pricing-system/

external.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is about to become a public company - and the move could impact Americans’ retirement accounts, some experts have warned. Last month, SpaceX unveiled plans to publicly sell its stock for the first time. The company’s initial public offering, expected June 12, is on track to be the largest in history at a projected $1.75 trillion and could put Musk on a path to becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Earlier this year, some of the major stock market indexes, including the Nasdaq where SpaceX will soon be available, changed their rules to allow high-value companies to join much quicker than normal after going public. That’s where Americans’ retirement accounts come in. Retirement account managers typically will invest in so-called index funds. Basically, those funds spread out money across a market such as the Nasdaq, so individual investors will have small amounts in lots of companies and not need to worry about trading stocks day-to-day. That means that millions of Americans could see portions of their 401Ks or other retirement accounts invested in the aerospace company founded by the world’s richest person. In light of the SpaceX IPO’s accelerated timeline, that causes concern among some tech and finance...

record.text: Autoroute A69: audience au Conseil d'État sur le recours des opposants le 15 juin

external.description:
Par AFP © 2026 AFP 


 Le Conseil d'État examinera le 15 juin le recours des opposants à l'autoroute A69 Toulouse-Castres, portant sur la validation de l'autorisation du chantier par la cour administrative d'appel de Toulouse, a-t-on appris de sources concordantes. "Il est l'heure pour le Conseil d'Etat de remettre le Tarn sur le Droit chemin", a affirmé le collectif anti-A69 La Voie est libre (LVEL), dans un communiqué annonçant la date de cette audience, prévue à 14h00 et confirmée à l'AFP par une source au sein de l'institution. "Le Conseil d'État n'aura d'autre choix que de corriger l'erreur de droit manifeste de la cour d'appel de Toulouse", estime le collectif. La plus haute juridiction administrative doit statuer sur la légalité de l'arrêt rendu le 30 décembre 2025 par la cour administrative d'appel de Toulouse qui a validé l'autorisation environnementale de ce chantier et permis la poursuite des travaux, toujours en cours entre Haute-Garonne et Tarn. La cour administrative était revenue sur un jugement du tribunal administratif de Toulouse qui, en février 2025, avait suspendu le projet d'autoroute faute de "raison impérative d'intérêt public majeur" (RIIPM), condition requise pour déroger à la protection des espèces protégées. Défendu comme un facteur de désenclavement du Tarn et en particulier du bassin urbain formé par les villes de Castres et de Mazamet, au sud du département, le chantier doit s'achever à la mi-octobre, selon le futur concessionnaire Atosca.

record.text: La Bourse de Paris salue la baisse du pétrole et les vieilles valeurs de l'innovation

external.description:
Par AFP © 2026 AFP 


 La Bourse de Paris a progressé jeudi, portée par la baisse du pétrole et la revanche de deux valeurs anciennes de l'innovation, qui ont profité d'un coup de semonce sur l'intelligence artificielle à New York. L'indice CAC 40 a progressé de presque 100 points (93,87, +1,15%) à 8.244,29 points. La veille, le panier des 40 plus grandes valeurs boursières françaises avait cédé 0,71%, pour terminer à 8.150,42 points. Capgemini (services numériques, +6,61% à 104,20 euros) et Dassault Systèmes (logiciels, +5,94% à 20,16 euros) ont réalisé la meilleure performance du jour. Inversement, les investisseurs ont vendu des titres de STMicroelectronics (semi-conducteurs, -2,58% à 66,72 euros), une valeur qui a fortement progressé depuis le début de l'année avec l'enthousiasme pour les infrastructures de l'intelligence artificielle (IA). Paris a ainsi traduit le recul au Nasdaq à New York du fabricant de semi-conducteurs Broadcom (près de -13% vers 17H30 GMT), interprété comme un coup de chaud sur le développement de l'IA. Broadcom a fortement dévissé faute d'avoir relevé ses objectifs annuels. "Les attentes des investisseurs sont élevées. Ils considèrent que la croissance, c'est bien, mais qu'il faut faire attention au prix de la croissance", décrypte Kevin Thozet, conseiller portefeuille pour Carmignac. Comme les autres places européennes, Paris a été portée par la baisse des prix du pétrole, alors qu'aucun progrès n'a été enregistré pour la réouverture du détroit d'Ormuz qui bloque 20% de la consommation mondiale, provoquant un "choc d'offre" inflationniste. Jeudi, la tonalité des discours était offensive. Le guide suprême iranien, Mojtaba Khamenei, a assuré que les Etats-Unis et Israël avaient subi un "revers cuisant" dans la guerre contre son pays, au moment où les négociations de paix entre Washington et Téhéran butent notamment sur la question du Liban. Vers 19H30, le Brent de la mer du Nord, référence en Europe reculait malgré tout fortement (-3.23%) à 94,65 dollars le baril. La baisse du WTI américain était encore plus accentuée (-3,80% à 92.37 euros). Trop peu, trop tard? L'agence Fitch a estimé que "la crise pétrolière assombrit les perspectives de croissance mondiale" et qu'elle revoyait ses prévisions à la baisse "de 0,2 point de pourcentage, à 2,4 %".
